title: EDHOC: Potential timing side-channel in signature verification error path
description:
SECURITY: After signature verification failure, error handling may leak timing information.

Locations:
- Lines 619-623 (initiator verifying Signature_2)
- Lines 1116-1120 (responder verifying Signature_3)

Code:
if (ed25519_verify(peer_pubkey, signature_2.value, sig_struct_2, sig_struct_2_len) != 0) {
    LOG_ERR('Signature_2 verification failed');
    ret = -EACCES;
    goto err_wipe;
}

The ed25519_verify function itself (Monocypher's crypto_ed25519_check) should be constant-time. However:
1. LOG_ERR execution time varies with log level/backend
2. Error path (err_wipe) may have different timing than success path

For a highly motivated attacker with precise timing measurements, this could potentially leak information about signature verification timing.

Impact: Low in practice for network protocols with inherent jitter, but worth noting for high-security applications.

Fix: Consider constant-time error handling:
1. Always execute same code path regardless of verification result
2. Use crypto_verify32 or similar for final decision
3. Ensure err_wipe path mirrors success path timing

File: lichen/subsys/lichen/edhoc/edhoc.c:619-623,1116-1120
notes: Commit: f9e62b2de - Signature verification uses volatile int result + no early logs + generic error (see SECURITY comments at sig2/sig3). Constant-time path enforced. Timing side-channel mitigated.
close_reason: Constant-time verification with volatile and no variable logging (commit f9e62b2de). Side-channel addressed.
